items.title: Man sentenced to 10 years in jail for stabbing death of niece in Darwin

children.content: Andrew Mungatopi has been sentenced to 10 years and two months in prison for the fatal stabbing of his niece in a Darwin backyard in 2024.

image.altText: A First Nations man wearing mirrored sunglasses, pointing at himself.

items.title: Three hospitalised as Central Australian family dispute turns violent

children.content: Three people have been hospitalised after a family dispute at a Central Australian outstation turned violent over the weekend, sparking fears of further unrest.

image.altText: Two women, looking serious, sitting on chairs inside a studio.

items.title: NT attorney-general backs community safety changes despite unrest

children.content: The government says bail law changes made a year ago are working despite a string of tragedies and unrest in bush communities.

gallery.altText: Close-up of a white woman, blonde hair, wearing a light blue blazer, white blouse, looking intently at camera, soft smile.

thumbnail.altText: Close-up of a white woman, blonde hair, wearing a light blue blazer, white blouse, looking intently at camera, soft smile.

items.title: 'National reluctance' to discuss abuse of Indigenous children, says ex-minister

children.content: A review has begun into the family circumstances of Kumanjayi Little Baby leading up to her disappearance and death, which relatives hope will find answers.

image.altText: An Indigenous woman with grey curly hair wearing a black top sitting in a living room.

items.title: Inpex seeks urgent orders to stop strike action in Darwin

children.content: Oil and gas giant Inpex has launched proceedings with the Fair Work Commission seeking urgent orders to stop strike action at its Ichthys LNG facility in Darwin.

image.altText: Inpex shipment

items.title: Police release body-worn footage of fugitive bikie's dramatic arrest

children.content: Northern Territory Police have released dramatic body-worn footage of the arrest of Mongols bikie Brandt Graham after the 44-year-old jumped a court dock last week, sparking a five-day manhunt.

image.altText: Nold_GrahamArrest_1006

articles.title: Mongols fugitive taken down by dog squad after fleeing traffic stop on foot

children.content: Police have arrested fugitive Top End Mongols bikie Brandt Graham five days after the 44-year-old escaped from custody last week, sparking an "extensive manhunt".

image.altText: A shirtless man in jeans with his face blurred is surrounded by three police officers who hold his arms

avatar.alt: A journalist wearing a button-up shirt, with a neutral facial expression.

items.title: Mining set to restart at one of the NT's biggest gold reserves

children.content: After being dormant for nearly 30 years, the Mount Todd gold mine near Katherine is gearing up to restart production. While there are calls for local Aboriginal people to have the "first crack" at mining jobs, the operator is planning to engage a largely FIFO workforce.

image.altText: A miner inspects a large rock.

items.title: Police charge trio with Darwin man's murder in alleged 'vicious attack'

children.content: Police have charged three men who allegedly beat 48-year-old Darwin man Martin Wiberg to death in a "vicious attack" at the Casuarina bus stop earlier this year with his murder.

image.altText: Man holds three kittens under his chin

items.title: Iron ore company 'undermines' promises after failing to pay traditional owners

children.content: Nathan River Resources went into administration and owes about $360 million to creditors including the NT government and traditional owners.

image.altText: A digger is filling up a truck with iron ore in the NT.

items.title: First Indigenous SANFL player David Kantilla inducted into hall of fame

children.content: Six new members are inducted into the Australian Football Hall of Fame, including Geelong and Gold Coast legend Gary Ablett Jr.

image.altText: Gary Ablett Jr in a tuxedo seen side-on with his name on a screen behind him.

items.title: 'Keyboard warriors': Northern Ireland leader hits out at Musk and Robinson

children.content: Billionaire Elon Musk and far-right activist Tommy Robinson have seized upon the alleged stabbing of a man in Belfast by a Sudanese immigrant to push an anti-immigration agenda.

image.altText: Two pictures of Tommy Robinson and Elon Musk

items.title: 'Trying not to cry': Rabbitohs rise after emotional Jai Arrow moment

children.content: Jai Arrow has inspired South Sydney to a commanding win over the Brisbane Broncos, on an emotional night at Stadium Australia. Follow live.

image.altText: Jai Arrow walks on to the field with his daughter Ayla Rae

items.title: 'Inspirational' Dawson stars as Crows send premiership statement

children.content: Jordan Dawson plays one of the best quarters of his career as Adelaide claims a 57-point win over the Western Bulldogs at Docklands.

image.altText: Jordan Dawson celebrates a goal with Adelaide teammates

items.title: 'Passing the baton': RecipeTin Eats founder adopts new canine companion

children.content: Nagi Maehashi, the founder of RecipeTin Eats, introduces her new canine companion Jaffle.

image.altText: Nagi, smiling, holds up a golden retriever puppy's arms

items.title: Are YouTubers taking over the film industry?

children.content: YouTube is changing the film industry. It's a place where young filmmakers can find and build an audience.
More and more YouTube stars are making the jump into mainstream films.
So, are YouTubers are taking over the film industry?

items.title: Rosemarie Campbell's family responds to damning inquest findings

children.content: The family of a Gold Coast woman who died following complications from a surgery performed by Reza Adib have called for accountability and meaningful change in the wake of damning inquest findings.

image.altText: A selfie of Rosemarie Campbell.

items.title: Man charged over alleged harassment of Bondi royal commission witness

children.content: The man from northern NSW was also found in possession of neo-Nazi material during a search warrant on Thursday.

image.altText: two officers walking into a backyard property with a dog.

items.title: What time does the FIFA World Cup kick-off, Australian time?

children.content: Check out when your team's matches are kicking off at the 2026 FIFA World Cup so you don't miss a match.

items.title: Thai court sentences two Uyghur men to death for 2015 Bangkok bombing

children.content: The men were accused of carrying out the attack on the popular Erawan Shrine that killed 20 people, many of whom were Chinese tourists.

image.altText: Police officers in coveralls stand among debris around a Hindu shrine.

items.title: State disability ministers lash NDIS overhaul

children.content: State and territory disability ministers have accused the Albanese government of pushing on with NDIS reform that does not align with previous agreements.

image.altText: A man in a suit and tie looks sternly ahead.

items.title: In Moldova, I met a woman with a dangerous plan to save her life

children.content: On a park bench in Moldova, I meet a Ukrainian refugee who is planning to return home through a war zone for cancer treatment. We talk for hours.

image.altText: A close up of Tatiana, who has short platimun blonde hair.

items.title: Steven Spielberg returns to sci-fi and alien wonder in Disclosure Day

children.content: ET director Steven Spielberg dives back into the extra terrestrial with his latest film while asking 'How do you astonish an audience when anything is possible, and nothing is real?

image.altText: A woman holds a glowing rod in front of a group of people

items.title: Ebola outbreak grows to almost 600 confirmed cases

children.content: In just two weeks the number of confirmed Ebola virus cases has increased more than eightfold, with at least 100 people now dead.

gallery.altText: A person in scrubs applies a mask to a person in full PPE.

thumbnail.altText: A person in scrubs applies a mask to a person in full PPE.

items.title: BHP bracing for strike after WA workers back industrial action

children.content: Unionised workers at BHPs operations in Port Hedland could walk off the job as soon as next week after endorsing protected industrial action.

image.altText: A water tank with the words 'Welcome to Port Hedland' and the BHP logo

items.title: 'Fugitive' ex-bikie detained in Bali found hiding in private jet toilet

children.content: Former bikie boss Angelo Pandeli was attempting to fly to Africa on a fake Brazilian passport when Indonesian immigration authorities apprehended him over an Interpol notice.

image.altText: Man in sunglasses and khaki shirt with stairs in the background

items.title: 'Just chill, relax': Infantino defends World Cup visa issues, ticket prices

children.content: FIFA president Gianni Infantino defends soccer's governing body's handling of visa issues and ticket prices ahead of the World Cup.

articles.title: Injury concern for Socceroos star on eve of World Cup

children.content: Socceroos striker Mo Toure is a no-show at the beginning of a scheduled training session, bringing his fitness into doubt just days out from their World Cup opener against Türkiye.

image.altText: Mo Toure looks sad during a game for the Socceroos.

articles.title: Mexico City welcomes World Cup but reality bites for many

children.content: As Mexicans prepare to host or co-host the FIFA World Cup for a record third time, many locals are frustrated by being locked out of the tournament, while others are more optimistic.

image.altText: A woman in Catrina make up holding a fake world cup trophy poses with Mexico football fans

gallery.altText: FIFA president Gianni Infantino looks at a questioner at a press conference with the World Cup next to him.

thumbnail.altText: FIFA president Gianni Infantino looks at a questioner at a press conference with the World Cup next to him.

items.title: Child detainees in Queensland allegedly locked down for entire weekend

children.content: Children incarcerated at two south-east Queensland youth detention facilities were allegedly locked down for an entire weekend due to staff shortages that have prompted dozens of workers across the state to walk off the job.

image.altText: West Moreton and Brisbane Youth Detention Centre.

items.title: Government flags risk of relying on Elon Musk's SpaceX and Starlink

children.content: As tens of thousands of Australians line up to buy shares in Elon Musk's SpaceX blockbuster public offering, Australian government officials are privately flagging the risks posed by the country's growing reliance on its satellite internet service, Starlink.

image.altText: Elon Musk

items.title: Taylor rules out One Nation seat carve-up as party raises $2m

children.content: Opposition leader Angus Taylor says the Coalition has no plan to share the seats the party targets at the next federal election with One Nation as Pauline Hanson's party says it has raised $2 million since launch.

image.altText: An older man wearing a suit gives an impassioned speech.

items.title: World's biggest whale graveyard found in Indian Ocean off Australia

children.content: Hundreds of whale fossils, including those from extinct species new to science, range in age from recent to 5 million years old and are also home to undescribed deep-sea creatures.

image.altText: Underwater vision of a whale fossil on the seafloor.

items.title: Grandfather's fall inspires 20yo student's smart clock invention

children.content: After it took nearly two days for his grandfather to be found following a serious fall, Kai Gould has created a device to connect older people living independently with their loved ones.

gallery.altText: Older man and younger man holding clock
Man with hands working on electrical board

thumbnail.altText: Older man and younger man holding clock
Man with hands working on electrical board

items.title: 'Very unfair': Collapsed petrol empire lands deal to pay fraction of $13m debt

children.content: The directors of Zoya Investments have agreed to a $2 million settlement to end the liquidator's case against them.

image.altText: Rizwan Rana in a Ralph Lauren polo shirt standing in front of his Lexus.

items.title: Culture in Australia's largest police force 'unacceptable'

children.content: Bullying, discrimination and sexual harassment are occurring at "unacceptable levels" within the NSW Police Force and employees are too afraid to speak up, an independent report has found.

image.altText: Uniformed Police officers survey a busy protest

items.title: Pope Leo blesses Barcelona's Sagrada Familia as an 'architectural masterpiece'

children.content: The pontiff has celebrated Spain's Sagrada Familia Basilica as a masterpiece of "stones, colours and light," as he marked the centenary of the death of its architect Antoni Gaudí.

gallery.altText: The interior hall of the Sagrada Familia illuminated in sunlight between its pylons, over a crowd of people.
The Sagrada Familia at night, illuminted in light with a white cross at its top, surrounded by people in white robes.
Pope Leo and Catholic Church clergy members in white and gold robes standing under large organs and a sculpture of Jesus.

thumbnail.altText: The interior hall of the Sagrada Familia illuminated in sunlight between its pylons, over a crowd of people.
The Sagrada Familia at night, illuminted in light with a white cross at its top, surrounded by people in white robes.
Pope Leo and Catholic Church clergy members in white and gold robes standing under large organs and a sculpture of Jesus.
